reasoning: The knowledge cutoff is November 9, 2025, leaving only about seven weeks until the end of the year. The market estimate as of October 30, 2025, was exactly 5%. Closing or severely restricting the Strait of Hormuz—even if limited to Western shipping—would represent an extreme, likely suicidal escalation by Iran, as it would disrupt global oil markets and invite substantial military retaliation from the US and international coalitions. While regional tensions periodically spike and Iran has seized individual tankers in the past, a true blockade or severe restriction of oil traffic is historically exceptionally rare, and the brief two-month horizon makes the likelihood very low. Therefore, relying heavily on the recent market estimate of 0.05 is the most well-calibrated approach.
